channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Federal student loans will move to Treasury, further shrinking Education Department
item.description: The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Cursive is back. But should students be learning the skill?
item.description: A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?
item.content:encoded:
A Virginia after-school cursive club went viral. More than two dozen states require cursive in their curriculums. Is it an effective learning tool or just nostalgia?

(Image credit: Anna Rose Layden for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.content:encoded: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: Key oversight helping keep student loan records accurate has stopped, a watchdog says
item.description: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.content:encoded: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Texas swim team raises money for hospitalized high school custodian
item.description: A high school swim team in Texas raised nearly $20,000 to help their beloved school custodian after he spent months in the hospital.
item.content:encoded: A high school swim team in Texas raised nearly $20,000 to help their beloved school custodian after he spent months in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: How colleges are reconnecting with students who left before earning degrees
item.description: More than 43 million Americans have some college credit but no degree. Institutions have been partnering with a national organization to help those students re-enroll and finish their credential.
item.content:encoded: More than 43 million Americans have some college credit but no degree. Institutions have been partnering with a national organization to help those students re-enroll and finish their credential.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Carapezza

item.title: Teachers across California are considering work stoppages
item.description: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.content:encoded: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.dc:creator: Farida Romero

item.title: March (Audio) Madness! Here are the finalists in NPR's College Podcast Challenge
item.description: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Steve Drummond

item.title: Announcing the 2025 NPR College Podcast Challenge Honorable Mentions
item.description: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Student Podcast Challenge

item.title: College students, professors are making their own AI rules. They don't always agree
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.content:encoded: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines
